message: chore(beads): sync issue tracking state (bd-bqd1 closed)

files.patch:
@@ -144,7 +144,7 @@ {"id":"bd-2x0.7","title":"Implement JSON tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement JSON tokenizer ## Context JSON is ubiquitous for configuration and data exchange. It's also one of the simplest languages to tokenize, making it a good starting point and reference for testing the highlighting system. ## JSON Syntax (Complete Specification) JSON has exactly these token types: ### Structural Characters ``` { } [ ] : , ``` ### Literals ```json "string" // Strings (always double-quoted) 123 // Numbers (integer) -456 // Negative numbers 3.14 // Decimal numbers 1e10 // Scientific notation true // Boolean false // Boolean null // Null ``` ### String Escapes ```json \" \\ \/ \b \f \n \r \t \uXXXX ``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/json.rs`: ```rust pub struct JsonTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for JsonTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "JSON" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["json", "jsonc", "json5"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // JSON is line-based for most purposes // Strings technically can't span lines in strict JSON // But we handle JSONC (with comments) and JSON5 (with multi-line) } } ``` ### Token Mapping | JSON Element | TokenKind | |-------------|-----------| | `"key":` (object key) | Identifier or String | | `"value"` | String | | `\n`, `\t`, etc. | StringEscape | | `123`, `-3.14` | Number | | `true`, `false` | Boolean | | `null` | Constant | | `{`, `}`, `[`, `]` | Punctuation | | `:`, `,` | Delimiter | | `//` comment (JSONC) | Comment | | `/* */` comment (JSONC) | CommentBlock | ## Edge Cases - Distinguishing object keys from string values (keys are followed by `:`) - Unicode escapes (`\uXXXX`) - Scientific notation (`1e-10`) - JSONC comment support - Invalid JSON (highlight as Error) ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_json_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_numbers() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_booleans_null() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_structure() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_escapes() { ... } #[test] fn test_jsonc_comments() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/json.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All JSON value types highlighted correctly - [ ] Object keys distinguished from string values - [ ] String escapes highlighted - [ ] Numbers with all formats (int, float, scientific, negative) - [ ] JSONC comment support - [ ] Invalid syntax highlighted as Error - [ ] Test coverage >90% (JSON is simple enough for high coverage)","notes":"## Performance Requirements Same as all tokenizers (per AGENTS.md): - **Zero allocations per token** - Use &str slices into source - **<1μs per line** for typical JSON - JSON is simpler than Rust, so performance should be even better ## Testing with Tracing Per bd-2x0.11: - All tests use tracing (info!, debug!) - Run: RUST_LOG=debug cargo test json_tokenizer -- --nocapture ## Note on JSONC Support JSONC (JSON with Comments) is common in VS Code configs. Support single-line `//` and block `/* */` comments, but mark the file extension separately (.jsonc) to not confuse strict JSON parsers.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:45:17.329963585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:12:13.051156990Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:12:13.051138806Z","close_reason":"Implemented JSON tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2x0.8","title":"Implement TOML tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement TOML tokenizer ## Context TOML (Tom's Obvious Minimal Language) is the standard configuration format for Rust projects (Cargo.toml). Since OpenTUI is a Rust library, excellent TOML support is particularly relevant. ## TOML Syntax Elements ### Structure ```toml [section] [section.subsection] [[array.of.tables]] key = value "quoted key" = value dotted.key = value ``` ### Values ```toml # Strings string = "basic" multiline = """ multi line """ literal = 'no \escapes' literal_multiline = ''' multi line ''' # Numbers integer = 42 hex = 0xDEADBEEF octal = 0o755 binary = 0b11010110 float = 3.14 infinity = inf nan = nan # Boolean bool = true # Dates and times date = 2024-01-15 time = 14:30:00 datetime = 2024-01-15T14:30:00Z datetime_local = 2024-01-15T14:30:00 # Arrays array = [1, 2, 3] nested = [[1, 2], [3, 4]] # Inline tables inline = { key = "value", other = 42 } ``` ### Comments ```toml # Single line comment only key = "value" # End of line comment ``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/toml.rs`: ```rust pub struct TomlTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for TomlTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "TOML" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["toml"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle multi-line string continuation // Section headers [name] // Key = value pairs // Comments } } ``` ### Token Mapping | TOML Element | TokenKind | |-------------|-----------| | `[section]` | Type | | `[[array]]` | Type | | `key =` | Identifier | | `"string"` | String | | `42`, `0xFF` | Number | | `true`/`false` | Boolean | | `2024-01-15` | Number (or custom Date) | | `# comment` | Comment | | `=`, `,` | Operator | | `[`, `]`, `{`, `}` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling - Multi-line basic strings: `""" ... """` - Multi-line literal strings: `''' ... '''` ## Edge Cases - Dotted keys (`package.version`) - Bare keys vs quoted keys - Date/time literals (many formats) - Unicode in strings and keys - Inline tables vs regular tables ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_toml_sections() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_key_values() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_multiline_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_numbers() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_dates() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_arrays() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_inline_tables() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/toml.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Section headers `[name]` highlighted as Type - [ ] Keys highlighted as Identifier - [ ] All string variants (basic, literal, multiline) - [ ] Numbers including special formats (hex, date/time) - [ ] Comments highlighted - [ ] Multi-line strings work correctly - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization - return spans not owned strings. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. TOML is critical for Cargo.toml editing use cases.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:45:50.867576492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:15:42.748398045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:15:42.748379901Z","close_reason":"Implemented TOML tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2x0.9","title":"Implement Markdown tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement Markdown tokenizer ## Context Markdown is the de facto standard for documentation, READMEs, and notes. Terminal-based editors and tools frequently work with Markdown, making it a high-value tokenizer to include. ## Markdown Syntax Elements ### Headings ```markdown # Heading 1 ## Heading 2 ### Heading 3 (up to 6) Alternative Heading ================== Alternative H2 -------------- ``` ### Emphasis ```markdown *italic* or _italic_ **bold** or __bold__ ***bold italic*** ~~strikethrough~~ ``` ### Code ```markdown `inline code` indented code block (4 spaces) ```language fenced code block ``` ``` ### Links and Images ```markdown [link text](url) [link text](url "title") [reference link][ref] [ref]: url ![alt text](image.png) ![alt text](image.png "title") ``` ### Lists ```markdown - Unordered item * Also unordered + Also unordered 1. Ordered item 2. Ordered item - [ ] Task item unchecked - [x] Task item checked ``` ### Blockquotes ```markdown > Quote line > Continued quote ``` ### Horizontal Rules ```markdown --- *** ___ ``` ### Tables (GFM) ```markdown | Header | Header | |--------|--------| | Cell | Cell | ``` ### HTML ```markdown
Raw HTML
``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs`: ```rust pub struct MarkdownTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for MarkdownTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "Markdown" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["md", "markdown", "mkd", "mkdn"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle fenced code block continuation // Line-start elements: headings, lists, blockquotes, HR // Inline elements: emphasis, code, links } } ``` ### Token Mapping | Markdown Element | TokenKind | |-----------------|-----------| | `# Heading` | Heading | | `*italic*` | Emphasis | | `**bold**` | Emphasis + Bold style | | `[text](url)` | Link | | `![alt](url)` | Link | | `` `code` `` | CodeInline | | ``` ```code``` ``` | CodeBlock | | `> quote` | Comment (or custom Quote) | | `- item` | Punctuation + Text | | `---` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling 1. **Fenced code blocks** - ``` ... ``` (track the fence marker) 2. **Indented code blocks** - 4+ spaces at line start 3. **Multi-line blockquotes** - Lines starting with `>` ## Edge Cases - Escaping: `\*not italic\*` - Nested emphasis: `***bold and italic***` - Links with parentheses in URL - Code spans with backticks inside: ``` `` `code` `` ``` - Setext headings (underline style) - HTML blocks ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_md_headings() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_emphasis() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_inline() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_blocks() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_links() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_lists() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_blockquotes() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_escaping() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Headings (ATX and Setext styles) - [ ] Emphasis (italic, bold, strikethrough) - [ ] Inline and fenced code - [ ] Links and images - [ ] Lists (ordered, unordered, task) - [ ] Blockquotes - [ ] Fenced code block continuation - [ ] Escape sequences - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. Markdown is high-value for documentation editing in terminal apps.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:46:23.879677476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704966007Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704947462Z","close_reason":"Implemented Markdown tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} -{"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation. - demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks. - Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:08.399425192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation. - demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks. - Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:01:39.717967202Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-313","title":"Expand benchmark coverage beyond buffer module","description":"Currently only benches/buffer.rs exists. Add benchmarks for: renderer diff detection, color blending operations, text buffer operations, unicode width calculations.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:46.184813379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.657047735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.656970329Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive benchmarks added for color blending (creation, blending, conversion, interpolation), text buffer (TextBuffer, EditBuffer, cursor movement, undo/redo), renderer diff detection (identical, single change, row change, scattered, full diff), and unicode width calculations (ASCII, CJK, emoji, grapheme operations). All benchmarks compile and run successfully.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds) ## Purpose The flagship demo should be discoverable. Add a README section that explains: - how to run the demo - what it demonstrates - essential keybinds - tour mode and headless smoke mode - recommended terminals for best visuals ## Content Requirements Add a `Demo Showcase` section to `README.md`: Run: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase Tour (unattended): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour Headless smoke: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke Headless JSON dump (for tests/debugging): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json Degradation matrix (example manual run): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --headless-size 50x15 --cap-preset minimal Document keybinds (or point to the in-app help overlay) and call out terminal recommendations (kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty) for best results. ## Flag Summary (Must Match bd-2iv) Include a small, explicit list of supported flags (grouped by category): Interactive: - --fps - --tour - --exit-after-tour - --max-frames Headless/testing: - --headless-smoke - --headless-dump-json - --headless-check - --headless-size Terminal behavior: - --no-mouse - --no-alt-screen - --no-cap-queries Capability override: - --cap-preset Advanced: - --threaded - --seed ## Acceptance Notes - README makes it obvious the demo exists and how to run it. - Flags documented match the actual CLI (bd-2iv).","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `README.md` contains a clear `Demo Showcase` section with run commands (interactive, tour, headless). - The documented flag list matches the implemented CLI (`bd-2iv`) including headless size + capability preset controls. - The README points users to the in-app help overlay for the authoritative keybind list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498373446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498306461Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-35e","title":"demo_showcase UI: toast + notification system (stack + fade)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Toast + Notification System (Stack + Fade) ## Purpose Toasts make the demo feel like a real application and also give us a constant excuse to show: - translucent overlays (opacity stack) - animated alpha fades (time-driven) - crisp typography + spacing They also provide immediate feedback when commands/actions occur. ## Behaviors - Toasts appear in a consistent corner (e.g., bottom-right above the status bar). - Multiple toasts stack with a small vertical gap. - Each toast has: - severity: Info / Warn / Error - title + optional detail line - TTL (auto-dismiss) with fade-out - optional “action” hint (e.g., `R` retry) ## Rendering Notes - Background should be semi-transparent with a gradient edge or subtle border. - Severity affects accent color and icon glyph. - Clip the toast region with scissor so it never draws into the status bar. ## Integration Points Toasts should be produced by: - command palette executions - tour step transitions - simulated preview errors/retries - clipboard/link actions Implementation suggestion: - `struct Toast { created_at, ttl, level, title, detail, ... }` - `struct ToastManager { toasts: VecDeque }` - render in the main render pass after panels but before full-screen overlays.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - Toasts render as translucent cards with consistent spacing, typography, and severity styling. - Toasts stack with a gap and auto-dismiss after TTL with a smooth fade. - Toast region is clipped so it never draws into the status bar. - Core actions (palette commands, tour steps, paste, resize, simulated preview error) generate toasts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.894150944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:16:13.720258754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:16:13.720185457Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.676575823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203719095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.906489096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.094359519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
